evals.prompt: 帮我写一个训练脚本，用 PyTorch 训练一个简单的 ResNet18 做 CIFAR-10 分类，用 cosine annealing scheduler，跑 50 个 epoch，用本机 GPU 跑就行
evals.expected_output: Should produce: (1) a config.yaml with all params including lr, batch_size, epochs, scheduler type; (2) recording field negotiation asking user about loss, accuracy, lr, grad_norm, throughput, gpu_memory; (3) a pre-flight summary; (4) training script that writes scalars.csv in long format and events.jsonl; (5) proper exp/ directory structure with snapshot

assertions.text: Generates a config.yaml with run.name, run.task_type, backend section, params section, and recording section
Proactively asks the user what metrics to record AND proposes additional fields like grad_norm, throughput, gpu_memory_allocated
Shows a pre-flight summary with all scalar fields, per-item fields, and asks for confirmation before proceeding
Training script writes scalars.csv with columns: timestamp,step,phase,field,value (long format)
Training script flushes after every write to scalars.csv
Output directory follows the naming convention {name}_{YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS}_{identifier}
No hardcoded hyperparameters in the training script — all come from config

evals.prompt: I have a script eval_model.py that evaluates a language model on a benchmark. I want to run it on the cluster via Slurm. It takes --model_path and --dataset arguments. I want to evaluate on 3 datasets: gsm8k, math, humaneval. Help me set up the experiment.
evals.expected_output: Should produce: (1) config.yaml with eval-specific params; (2) negotiate recording fields proposing accuracy, per-sample prediction/ground_truth, latency, confidence; (3) Slurm job script with proper SBATCH headers; (4) pre-flight check; (5) proper directory structure

assertions.text: Detects Slurm backend or asks user about it since they mentioned 'cluster'
Config includes task_type: eval and covers all three datasets (either in one config or separate per-dataset configs)
Proposes eval-specific recording fields: primary metric (accuracy/score), per-sample predictions, latency, confidence scores
Generates a valid Slurm script with #SBATCH headers matching config backend section
Slurm script includes code snapshot, config copy, and run_manifest.json creation
Shows pre-flight summary before generating the job script

evals.prompt: 写个数据处理脚本，把 /data/raw/wiki_dump.jsonl 里的文章做 tokenize 和过滤（去掉太短的），输出到一个新的 jsonl 文件。大概有 500 万条数据，可能要跑几个小时
evals.expected_output: Should produce: (1) config with task_type: process; (2) negotiate recording fields proposing items_processed, items_filtered, filter_reason, tokens_per_doc, processing_time_ms, output_size_bytes; (3) incremental writing; (4) proper exp/ structure; (5) suggest tmux for long-running local task

assertions.text: Config has task_type: process with input_file and output_file in params
Proposes data-processing specific fields: items_processed, items_skipped/filtered, filter_reason, processing_time_per_item, output_size
Script writes output incrementally (not buffered until end)
Script records progress to scalars.csv (items_processed count over time)
Launcher script or comments include tmux/background execution guidance for long-running local tasks
Shows pre-flight summary including all recording fields
